methods.name: Stage-Progression
methods.rationale: Applies typical Series B → next-stage step-up multiplier (1.9x) to $1.8B last post money.

methods.name: Peer-Median
methods.rationale: Median across 4 same-sector + same-stage peers in Biotech & Health. Range: $600M - $3.3B.

methods.skip_reason: target revenue not on file (private companies rarely disclose)
methods.rationale: Public Biotech & Health P/S median is 7.8x across 3 tickers. Cannot apply without target's revenue.

data.narrative:
Neko Health is a Series B biotech company founded by Daniel Ek and Hjalmar Nilsonne with $326M+ in total raised and a last post-money valuation of $1.8B. The valuation analysis draws on a comparable universe of eight private-stage companies in Biotech & Health, including Eikon Therapeutics, Asimov, and Profluent, plus three public sector benchmarks (RXRX, TEM, ONT.L).

Two quantitative methods proved applicable. Stage-progression analysis applies a typical 1.9x step-up multiplier from Series B to the next funding stage, implying a valuation of $3.4B. Peer-median anchoring across four same-stage, same-sector comparables—with valuations ranging from $600M to $3.3B—yields a median of $805M. The public-comp approach using a sector P/S median of 7.8x cannot be applied due to absence of disclosed revenue.

The triangulated range spans $684M (low) to $3.9B (high), with a median of $2.1B. The substantial dispersion reflects the high variance within the peer set, particularly the $3.3B outlier (Eikon Therapeutics) versus the $600-810M cluster. Material uncertainties include the target's unrevealed revenue, clinical stage, and whether recent progress since the $1.8B round justifies progression-based upside. The median estimate of $2.1B sits materially above the last post-money but meaningfully below stage-progression upside.
